violations.text: Menu lacks an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sinks in the kitchen and both bars. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen and both bars were lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC discarded ice and turned off unit to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

A variety of items (rice, chicken, sauce), which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Items discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Curry sauce was observed past the 7 day date marking. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Steak and lamb are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed various items still cooling in the 2 door cooler from the night before. Items were observed in containers greater than four inches deep, with plastic wrap, lid closed, and stacked together. Items were discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

ROP Tuna was observed being thawed with the package still intact in residential cooler. ROP seafood must be removed from packaging prior to thawing in a cooler. PIC discarded item. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Foil wrapped cardboard is used in the construction of dividers in the main bar chest cooler. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sinks in the kitchen and both bars. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Hand washing sinks at both bars lack hot water. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Food prep sink in kitchen lacks an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
Inspector observed filled trash bags, cardboard, and other debris around dumpster area. Area to be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

The following was observed in poor repair. - main bar wall (under bar counter FRP was removed/broken) - floor/wall joint near mop sink (hole observed and missing coving) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as sauces and beverage syrups) in the 2 door refrigerator and walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/18/2023
